Description: Miniopterus schreibersii genome assembly, chromosome: 15
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Chiroptera; Yangochiroptera; Miniopteridae; Miniopterus
Assembly: On Apr 5, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ071107.1.; The assembly mMinSch1.hap1.2 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Miniopterus schreibersii (Schreibers' long-fingered bat) GCA_964145185.1. The order and orientation of contigs along the Y chromosome is uncertain between 6.6 Mb and 15.5 Mb.

Description: Coleophora obscenella genome assembly, chromosome: 9
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Gelechioidea; Coleophoridae; Coleophora
Assembly: The assembly ilColObse1.hap1.1 is based on 91x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z, W1 and W2 were identified by copy number in the diploid genome and by HiC signal. The Z chromosome appears to have gone through a recent fusion between the canonical Z chromosome and an ancestral autosome. This has also resulted in two W chromosomes being present in the genome.

Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. 'Chromosome Z identified by identification of ancestral Z BUSCO genes' Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes.
